title: Garlicky Silky Turkish Rice Pilaf
description: A fragrant, silky rice pilaf with a punch of garlic and a subtle splash of rice wine, perfect for a quick vegetarian Turkish-inspired meal.
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.
In a medium saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and cinnamon; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the rinsed rice and toast it lightly, stirring to coat with the spice mixture, for 2 minutes.
Pour in the rice wine and let it evaporate completely, stirring constantly.
Add the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let the rice simmer for 15 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed.
Remove from heat, keep the lid on, and let it steam for an additional 5 minutes.
Fluff the rice with a fork, stir in the optional butter if using, then sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
